search:unit-testing-javascript:javascrip.content:
- **CLI Commands**: `npm install --save-dev jest`
- **Latest Version**: Jest 29.5
- **Key Config Options**:
  - `testEnvironment`: "jsdom" for DOM testing.
  - `transformIgnorePatterns`: ["node_modules/(?!(react|react-dom)/)"] to include React and ReactDOM in transformation.
  - `setupFilesAfterEnv`: ["./setupTests.js"] for global setup.

search:unit-testing-javascript:best prac.content:
- Use Jasmine for JavaScript unit testing.
- Install Jasmine via npm (`npm install jasmine-core --save-dev`).
- Run tests using the command `npx jasmine`.
- Ensure realistic input data in tests.
- Focus on testing behavior rather than implementation details.
- Utilize mocking and stubbing to isolate dependencies.
- Write tests for edge cases and error handling.
- Use coverage tools like Istanbul (`npm install nyc --save-dev`) to measure test effectiveness.

- **CLI Commands**: 
  - `npm install --save-dev jest`
  - `npx jest`

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Jest: [Check latest version on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/jest)

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `"testEnvironment": "node"` for Node.js environments
  - `"transform": {"^.+\\.jsx?$": "babel-jest"}` for JSX files
  - `"setupFilesAfterEnv": ["./setupTests.js"]` for global setup

search:unit-testing-javascript:javascrip.content:
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `npm install --save-dev jest`
  - `npx jest`

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Jest: 29.x

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `testEnvironment`: "jsdom" for DOM testing.
  - `transformIgnorePatterns`: ["node_modules/(?!(your-package-name))"] to include specific packages in transformation.
  - `setupFilesAfterEnv`: ["./jest.setup.js"] for global setup after environment is set up.

search:Unit Testing JavaScript:javascrip.content:
- **CLI Commands**:
  - `npm install --save-dev jest`
  - `npx jest`

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Jest: [Check latest version on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/jest)

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `testEnvironment`: Set to `"node"` or `"jsdom"`
  - `transformIgnorePatterns`: Array of regex patterns that match files to ignore when transforming
  - `setupFilesAfterEnv`: Array of paths to modules which run before each test file

- **CLI Commands**: `npm install --save-dev jest`
- **Latest Version**: Jest 29.x
- **Key Config Options**:
  - `testEnvironment`: "jsdom" for DOM testing
  - `transformIgnorePatterns`: ["node_modules/(?!(axios|lodash))"]
  - `setupFilesAfterEnv`: ["./setupTests.js"]

search:Unit Testing JavaScript:mocha and.content:
- Install Mocha and Chai globally: `npm install -g mocha chai`
- Initialize a new Mocha project: `mocha init`
- Run tests with Mocha: `mocha test.js`
- Key config options in `mocha.opts`: 
  - `--reporter spec` (default reporter)
  - `--timeout 2000` (set timeout for tests)
  - `--require @babel/register` (for Babel support)
- Latest versions can be found on [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/)
- Chai styles: 
  - Should style: `expect(value).to.be.true`
  - Expect style: `assert.strictEqual(actual, expected)`
  - Assert style: `value.should.be.true`

search:Unit Testing JavaScript:jest for.content:
- Install Jest using npm: `npm install --save-dev jest`
- Run tests: `npx jest`
- Watch mode for continuous testing: `npx jest --watch`
- Configure Jest with a `jest.config.js` file
- Key config options:
  - `testEnvironment`: "jsdom" or "node"
  - `transformIgnorePatterns`: Array of regex patterns to ignore during transformation
  - `setupFilesAfterEnv`: Array of paths to modules that run after the test framework has been installed in the environment
- Latest version: Check on [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/jest) for the most current version.

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `npm install --save-dev jest`
  - `npx jest`

- **Latest Version**: Check [Jest GitHub](https://github.com/facebook/jest) for the latest version.

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `testMatch`: Array of glob patterns to match files for testing.
  - `setupFilesAfterEnv`: Array of paths to modules that run some code to configure or set up the testing environment before each test file in the suite is executed.
  - `transformIgnorePatterns`: An array of regexp pattern strings that are matched against all source file paths, while applying transforms.
